title: Designing, refining, and maintaining agent skills at Perplexity
description: Reveals how to architect more predictable and reliable AI agent behaviors through skill design frameworks. Directly applicable insights from a leading AI company on making agents more controllable and maintainable.

title: The psychological fine print of AI
description: Explores how AI technology is creating new cognitive biases and reshaping the minds of users in ways designers haven't fully catalogued. A critical examination of the psychological implications of AI adoption that every designer needs to understand.

title: When AI decides and human signs off
description: Addresses the core design challenge in high-stakes AI products: maintaining human decision-making authority while leveraging AI for support. Essential guidance for teams building AI systems where trust and accountability are non-negotiable.

title: The trick to designing agentic AI is learning how to think like a manager
description: Reveals that designing autonomous AI agents requires managers' principles of boundary-setting and trust-building. A paradigm shift for product teams moving beyond traditional UI patterns into agentic systems.

title: Pinning is not saving. Saving is not favoriting. Favoriting is not flagging.
description: Deep UX pattern research distinguishing between subtle but critical interaction models that users conflate. Practical guidance for designing clearer information management systems across digital products.

title: The five dimensions of craft
description: Outlines timeless principles that distinguish products people actively use from those they emotionally connect with. Essential reading for designers seeking to elevate product quality beyond functionality.

title: St. Augustine and AI's false promise
description: Uses ancient philosophy to deconstruct modern AI hype and expose the unrealistic promises driving product decisions. A thoughtful antidote to uncritical AI adoption that challenges designers to think more deeply about consequences.

title: You skipped the first question. Now you're adding AI.
description: Exposes how teams use AI to mask fundamental design and strategy gaps instead of solving root problems. A sobering reality check for product leaders considering AI features as shortcuts around proper discovery.
